content_of_post_to_answer: Why do some houses feel heavy or stressful the moment you walk through the door?
answer: A house feels heavy when it offers no clear 'permission to rest'—your brain keeps reading unfinished cues: clutter that implies delayed decisions, dim light that hides edges, objects without order. It isn't just mess; it's unresolved attention. Some spaces don't relax you because they keep asking low-level questions you didn't agree to answer.
